mapping.a_element: two competing strategy types (spite vs. fairness) in a mutation-selection stochastic evolutionary process
mapping.b_element: two competing sub-system types (damaged vs. intact, i.e. defector vs. cooperator) in a Moran-type process

mapping.a_element: stochastic Moran/mutation-selection dynamics driving one strategy to dominate
mapping.b_element: within-group Moran dynamics where 'damage begets damage' and one type spreads

brief_justification: Both rest on the generic base of stochastic evolutionary (Moran-type) dynamics between two competing types, but the defining machinery differs: paper A is an eco-evolutionary game-environment feedback loop in which a single self-renewable resource is coupled bidirectionally to strategy frequencies (resource growth rate sets the regime), whereas paper B is a two-level nested multi-level-selection process (particles within collectives, within- plus between-group selection via scaling limits). A has no nested collectives / group-selection tier and B has no environmental resource feedback, so no role-for-role mapping holds for those decisive components.
